title: solidago patula subsp. strictula
intro: Solidago patula subsp. strictula, also known as the strict goldenrod, is a species of flowering plant in the aster family. It has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and boost the immune system. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress and inflammation. Solidago patula subsp. strictula can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to support overall health and well-being.
